findings.detail: reference_context.md cites 'R6: mb_str_slice_full(s, start, stop, step) must support negative step...' as a key requirement of string-ops. VERIFIED: cclab/specs/crates/cclab-mamba/runtime/string-ops.md (lines 26-109) has only R1 (core methods), R2 (predicates), R3 (alignment), R4 (f-string), R5 (encoding). There is NO R6 in the spec. The implementation string_ops.rs does contain mb_str_slice_full (lines 99-147) and inline test comments reference R6, but the spec has not been updated yet. R6 is a FORWARD reference — the new requirement this change will add. The spec plan action is 'modify' with sections [overview, requirements, changes], which correctly signals that R6 will be authored as part of this change. Acceptable as forward context, but the reference context should make clear this requirement does not yet exist (e.g. label it 'planned R6' or 'to be added'). No blocking issue — spec plan is correctly structured to add it.

reference_context.md uses narrative key requirements for conformance (golden file JIT stdout comparison, mamba-xfail directive mechanics, fixture data_structures/string_edge_cases_xfail.py expects 'fedcba', cargo test -p cclab-mamba --test conformance_tests). VERIFIED: cclab/specs/crates/cclab-mamba/testing/conformance.md is the 'xfail-zero' prior change spec (id: xfail-zero) which does document the xfail mechanism in its Test Plan (lines 222-298). The fixture string_edge_cases_xfail.py is NOT one of the 34 xfails listed in R1-R11 of that spec, but the mechanism description is accurate. Better spec for this context would be test-harness.md (cclab/specs/crates/cclab-mamba/testing/test-harness.md, id: test-harness), which documents the # RUN: directive dispatch and fixture discovery directly. However, since conformance.md is the only spec that explicitly covers the mamba-xfail directive pattern, the reference is acceptable. Additional note: the xfail marker was already removed from string_edge_cases_xfail.py (file currently contains no # mamba-xfail line), so pre-clarification task (2) is already complete.

Devil's advocate check: pre-clarification scope is (1) runtime fix in string_ops.rs, (2) xfail test conversion, (3) spec update. The runtime fix is covered by string-ops spec; the test conversion is covered by the conformance spec. No uncovered area found. The spec plan only has one entry (string-ops.md modify), which is correct — the xfail fixture change is a test file edit, not a new spec.

string-ops.md is the home for all runtime/string_ops.rs operations (R1–R5 already cover split/join/strip/predicates/alignment/f-string/encoding). Adding R6 for mb_str_slice_full step semantics extends this existing logical unit. Source file is crates/cclab-mamba/src/runtime/string_ops.rs, spec path crates/cclab-mamba/runtime/string-ops.md — correct mirroring. No split needed.

string-reverse-slice-fix.note: Source path verified at cclab/specs/crates/cclab-mamba/runtime/string-ops.md (106 lines, R1-R5). Subfolder 'runtime/' is present. Sections [overview, requirements, changes] are appropriate for adding a new R6 requirement for mb_str_slice_full with negative step and absent-start/stop sentinel semantics. Path mirrors source structure (src/runtime/string_ops.rs → runtime/string-ops.md). Existing R1-R5 sections will not be duplicated. The spec plan also covers the only required change — no additional spec entries are needed for the xfail fixture removal (which is a test file edit, already done).

summary: PASS with one warning. The reference context correctly covers both affected areas (string-ops runtime and conformance testing). Relevance scores are appropriate. The spec plan is structurally sound: one modify entry on string-ops.md with a valid path, correct subfolder, and appropriate sections. Warning: the 'R6' requirement ID cited in the string-ops key requirements does not yet exist in the spec (verified: string-ops.md has R1-R5 only). R6 is a forward reference that this change will create — acceptable given the spec plan action is 'modify' with 'requirements' as a new section, but the reference context should ideally label it 'planned R6' to avoid ambiguity. Secondary observation: conformance.md (xfail-zero change spec) is the best available but not ideal spec for the xfail mechanism; test-harness.md would be a cleaner reference. Both are informational — the spec writing task can proceed.
